---
title: "3. Dynamical perspective"
subtitle: "Session 3.1 · Bifurcation analysis"
toc: false
---

Neural mass models are dynamical systems. Before fitting them to data it is
essential to understand their bifurcation structure: which parameter regions
produce oscillations, fixed points, or multistability. This chapter gives you
the tools to map that landscape.

## Session

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### [3 · Bifurcation analysis](03_bifurcation_analysis.qmd)
Use numerical continuation and phase-plane analysis to identify bifurcation
points in a neural mass model. Learn to interpret the results and choose
biologically plausible operating points for whole-brain simulations.
:::

:::

---

*See the [workshop agenda](../agenda/agenda.qmd).*
